## Type of change

- [ ] feat: Adding a new project to the list
- [ ] fix: Correcting a broken link, typo, or wrong description
- [ ] docs: Changes to README, CONTRIBUTING, or other documentation
- [ ] chore: Maintenance tasks (reformatting, reorganizing categories)
- [ ] remove: Removing a project from the list

## Checklist

Before submitting, confirm all of the following:

- [ ] The project is not already on the list
- [ ] The project has been actively maintained within the past 12 months (last commit check)
- [ ] The project has at least 50 GitHub stars
- [ ] The project is licensed under MIT, Apache-2.0, GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, LGPL-2.1, LGPL-3.0, or AGPL-3.0
- [ ] The source code is fully and publicly available
- [ ] No proprietary dependencies are required to run core functionality
- [ ] The entry follows the required format: `- [Project Name](https://github.com/owner/repo) - One sentence description.`
- [ ] The entry is placed in the most specific existing category
- [ ] The commit message follows the Conventional Commits specification
- [ ] The PR title follows the format `Add: Project Name`, `Fix: description`, etc.

## Entry being added or modified

Paste the exact line(s) added or changed in `README.md`:

```markdown

```

## Why does this project belong on the list?

Briefly explain what the project does and why it meets the inclusion criteria.

## Additional context

Any other information relevant to this PR (optional).
